subject: science
en_record_text:
Excretion is the process of removing waste products from the body.
 The waste products may even be harmful and hence, they have to be thrown out of the body.
 Breathing out carbon dioxide, urinating, and sweating are examples of excretion in animals.
 Plants release waste gases through pores (small holes) in their leaves.

subject: math
en_record_text: How much interest should I pay if I took Rs. 20,000?
ne_record_text: त्यसो भए मैले रु. 20,000 ऋण लिएँ भने कति ब्याज तिर्नु पर्यो?

subject: math
en_record_text:
You can find that by using unitary method. 
 While using unitary method, check the yearly interest rate.

 Find out, how much needs to be paid for Rs. 1 in 1 year.
 Then, see how much you have to pay for the total money.
 Let me show this in detail.
ne_record_text:
यो तपाईँले ऐकिक नियमको प्रयोग गरेर निकाल्न सक्नु हुन्छ। 
 ऐकिक नियमको प्रयोग गर्दा, वार्षिक ब्याज पनि जाँच्नुहोस्।

 रु. 1 को लागि 1 वर्षमा, कति तिर्नुपर्छ पत्ता लगाउनुहोस्।
 त्यसपछि, पुरै पैसाको लागि कति तिर्नुपर्छ हेर्नुहोस्।
 पर्खनु म यसको अझै वर्णन गर्छु।

subject: math
en_record_text:
Always start with the interest rate. (The interest rate will tell you how much you need to pay for Rs. 100 in 1 year.)
 The interest rate is 10% per year.
ne_record_text:
जहिले पनि ब्याजदरबाट सुरू गर्नुहोस् (ब्याजदरले रु. 100 को लागि 1 वर्षमा कति ब्याज तिर्नुपर्छ भन्ने कुरा जनाउँछ।)
 यहाँ ब्याजदर 10% प्रति वर्ष छ:
   त्यसैले रु. 100 को लागि तपाईँले एक वर्षमा रु. 10 तिर्नु पर्ने छ।

subject: math
en_record_text:
Use unitary method to find the interest for Rs. 1 in 1 year:
    For Rs. 1, you have to pay Rs. 10/100 after 1 year.
ne_record_text:
ऐकिक नियमको प्रयोग गरि रु. 1 को लागि 1 वर्षको ब्याज निकाल्नुहोस्।
   तपाईँले रु. 1 को लागि तपाईँले एक वर्षमा रु. 10/100 तिर्नु पर्ने छ।

subject: math
en_record_text:
Use unitary method to find the interest for Rs. 20,000 in 1 year:
    For Rs. 20000, you have to pay Rs. 10/100 × 20000 in 1 year.
ne_record_text:
यसैगरि ऐकिक नियमको प्रयोग गरि तपाईँले रु. 20,000 को 1 वर्षको ब्याज निकाल्न सक्नुहुन्छ:
   तपाईँले रु. 20,000 को लागि तपाईँले एक वर्षमा रु. (10/100) × 20,000 तिर्नु पर्ने छ।

subject: math
en_record_text:
Correct! It is Rs. 2000.

subject: math
en_record_text:
Interest rate 10% per year: 
 (We know that percentage (%) simply represents a number out of 100.) 
 So, 10% means: for every Rs. 100, we shall charge you Rs. 10 extra and per year means you have to pay it after one year.
ne_record_text:
10% प्रति वर्षको ब्याजदर: 
 (प्रतिशत(%) भनेको 100 भित्रको एउटा अङ्क हो।) 
 10% भनेको: हरेक रु. 100 मा हामी तपाईँबाट रु. 10 बढी लिने छौँ र 'प्रति वर्ष' भनेको तपाईँले ऋण फिर्ता नगरेसम्म हरेक वर्ष ब्याज तिर्नुपर्छ भन्नु हो।

subject: math
en_record_text: So I will need to pay Rs. 2000 extra after 1 year.
ne_record_text: त्यसोभए मैले एक वर्षपछि रु. 2,000 बढी तिर्नु पर्यो।

subject: math
en_record_text:
It’s simple. You need to apply unitary method here too. 
 That means, first you need to know the interest for 1 year and find the interest for other year. 
 We know that your interest for 1 year is Rs. 2000.
 The interest after 2 years will be Rs. 2000 × 2 = Rs. 4000.
ne_record_text:
सजिलै छ। यहाँ पनि तपाईँले ऐकिक नियमको प्रयोग गर्नुपर्ने छ। 
 पहिला तपाईँले एक वर्षको ब्याज निकाल्नु पर्ने छ र फेरि अर्को वर्षको पनि ब्याज निकाल्नु पर्ने छ। 
 एक वर्षको ब्याज रु. 2000 हो भनेर हामीलाई थाहा छ।
 त्यसोभए दुई वर्षपछिको ब्याज रु. 2000 X 2 = रु. 4000 हुन्छ।

subject: math
en_record_text:
#Time@                 #Interest@
 1 year             Rs. 2000
 2 years          Rs. 2000 × 2 = Rs. 4000
 3 years          Rs. 2000 × 3 = Rs. 6000
 4 years          Rs. 2000 × 4 = Rs. 8000
 5 years          Rs. 2000 × 5 = Rs. 10000

subject: math

subject: math
en_record_text:
1.Let’s start with the interest rate. The interest rate is 8%.
     For Rs. 100, you will get Rs. 8 extra in 1 year.
 2. Use unitary method to find out how much you will get for Rs. 1.
     For Rs. 1, you will get Rs. 8/100 extra in 1 year.
 3. But you have Rs. 2000.
     For Rs. 2000, you will get Rs. 8/100×2000 = Rs. 160 in 1 year.
ne_record_text:
1.ब्याजदरबाट सुरु गरौँ। यहाँ ब्याजदर प्रति वर्ष 8% छ।
      हरेक रु. 100 को, एक वर्षमा तपाईँले रु. 8 बढी पाउनु हुने छ।
 2.ऐकिक नियमको प्रयोग गरि रु. 1. को कति ब्याज पाइँन्छ निकालौँ?
     रु. 1 को, एक वर्षमा तपाईँले रु. 8/100 ब्याज पाउनु हुन्छ।
 3. तर तपाईँसँग पहिले देखि नै रु. 2000 छ।
     रु. 2000 को, एक वर्षमा तपाईँले रु. 8/100 X 2000 = रु. 160 पाउनु हुन्छ।

en_record_text: 1. Deficiency of vitamin A can cause night blindness. People with this disease cannot see at night (dark). Eating green vegetables, yellow fruits, fish and ghee can prevent us from getting this disease.
ne_record_text: १. भिटामिन 'A' को कमीले रतन्धो हुन सक्छ। रतन्धो लागेको मानिसले राती आँखा देख्न सक्दैनन्। हरियो तरकारी, पहेँलो फल, माछा र घ्यू नियमित खाएमा यो रोगबाट बच्न सकिन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: 2. Lack of protein and carbohydrates results in marasmus. When one has marasmus, the muscles get weaker. It can reduce a child to skin and bones. It causes significant loss of weight as well. Children must eat the foods that give energy and vitamins.
ne_record_text: २. प्रोटिन र कार्बोहाइड्रेटको कमीले सुकेनास हुन सक्छ। सुकेनास हुँदा शरीरको मांसपेशीहरू कमजोर हुँदै जान्छ। यसले गर्दा रोगीको शरीर सुकेर हाड र छाला मात्र बाँकी रहन्छ। यसकारण शरीरको तौल एकदम कम हुन जान्छ। त्यसैले बालबालिकालाई प्रशस्त मात्रामा प्रोटिन र कार्बोहाइड्रेट दिने खानेकुराहरू दिनुपर्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: 3. Lack of protein in diet can have negative effects on a child’s growth. It can cause swelling of the body. It can easily be treated with a change in diet. All we have to eat is protein rich vegetables and fruits regularly.
ne_record_text: ३. प्रोटिनको कमीले शरीरको विकासमा नराम्रो असर पर्न सक्छ। यसले गर्दा शरीर सुन्निन पनि सक्छ। हामीले दिनदिनै खानेकुरा बदल्दै खाँदा यो समस्या सजिलै समाधान हुन सक्छ। हामीले प्रशस्त मात्रामा प्रोटिन भएको तरकारी र फलफूल नियमित रूपमा खानुपर्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: 4. Lack of nutrition can cause anemia, which means lack of blood in the body. People with this disease have pale skin, weakness and shortness of breath. Eating spinach, coriander leaves, fish, meat (kidney), mustard leaves, etc. can be helpful to treat this disease.
ne_record_text: ४. पौष्टिक तत्वको कमीले रक्तअल्पता (शरीरमा रगत कम हुने रोग) हुन सक्छ। यो रोग लागेको मानिसको छाला पहेँलो हुन्छ। शरीर कमजोर र श्वास पनि कम हुन्छ। सागपात, धनियाँ, माछा, मासु (कलेजो), रायोको साग आदि नियमित खाएमा यो रोग निको हुन सक्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text:
Protozoa 
 The organisms that belong to this group are called single celled organisms because they are made up of only one cell. These organisms cannot be seen with bare eyes. 
 All the life processes in their body such as: eating, respiration, movement etc. are controlled by the single cell. 
 Reproduction takes place in them through binary fission. 
 These organisms are usually found on human bodies, sea water and in humid areas.
ne_record_text:
एककोषीय (Protozoa) 
 एउटा मात्र सूक्ष्म कोषले बनेको हुनाले यो समूहमा पर्ने प्राणीलाई एककोषीय जीव भनिन्छ। यिनीहरूलाई नाङ्गो आँखाले देख्न सकिँदैन । 
 यिनीहरूको शरीरमा सञ्चालन हुने सबै जीवन प्रक्रियाहरू जस्तै: खाना खाने, सास फेर्ने , एकबाट अर्को ठाउँमा सर्ने आदि काम एक मात्र कोषले नियन्त्रण गरेको हुन्छ। 
 यिनीहरूमा प्रजनन क्रिया कोषको विभाजनबाट हुन्छ । 
 यिनीहरू प्राय: मानिसको शरीर, समुन्द्रको पानी वा ओसिलो ठाउँमा पाइन्छन्।

subject: science

en_record_text: They reproduce by dividing themselves in half. This process is called binary fission. During this process, everything needed by the cell to live is divided equally.

en_record_text: In Latin language porus means ‘pore’ i.e. hole and fer means 'to carry'. Since organisms that belong to this group have holes all over their bodies, they are also called porifera (commonly known as sponges).
ne_record_text: ल्याटिन भाषामा #porus@ भनेको ‘pore’ अर्थात् छिद्र र #fer@ भनेको ‘bearing’ अर्थात् बोक्नु हो। त्यसैले शरीर भरी छिद्र नै छिद्र हुने भएकाले यिनीहरूलाई पोरिफेरा (वा स्पन्ज) भनिन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text:
Porifera 
 In Latin language #porus@ means ‘pore’ i.e. hole and #fer@ means 'to carry'. Since, organisms that belong to this group have holes all over their bodies, they are also called porifera (commonly known as sponges). 
 Their body is made up of many cells. 
 They do not have a head, eyes and limbs. 
 They are usually found in the saltwater of seas and oceans.
ne_record_text:
पोरिफेरा (Porifera) 
 ल्याटिन भाषामा porus भनेको ‘pore’ अर्थात् छिद्र र fer भनेको ‘bearing’ अर्थात् बोक्नु हो। त्यसैले शरीर भरी छिद्र नै छिद्र हुने भएकाले यिनीहरूलाई पोरिफेरा (वा स्पन्ज) पनि भनिन्छ। 
 धेरैओटा कोषहरू मिलेर यिनीहरूको शरीर बनेको हुन्छ। 
 यिनीहरूको शरीरमा टाउको, आँखा, खुट्टा आदिको विकास भएको हुँदैन। 
 यिनीहरू प्राय समुद्रको नुनिलो पानीमा बस्छन्।

subject: science
en_record_text:
Nemathelminthes 
 The organisms that belong to this group have flat bodies. 
 Their bodies are ribbon shaped. 
 They are parasites and live by sucking blood of other animals. 
 Tapeworm lives in the intestines of humans, cows, buffaloes and pigs. Similarly, liver fluke lives in the livers of cattle.
ne_record_text:
डोला किरा (Nemathelminthes) 
 यस समूहमा पर्ने जीवहरूको शरीर गोलो र लाम्चो आकारको हुन्छ। 
 यी जीवहरूको शरीरको अगाडी र पछाडीको भाग चुच्चो परेको हुन्छ। 
 यिनीहरूमा भाले र पोथी छुट्टिएका हुन्छन्। पोथीको तुलनामा भाले छोटो र सानो हुन्छ। यिनीहरूको मेलद्वारा नै प्रजनन हुन्छ। 
 यिनीहरूको शरीरमा मुख र मलद्वार हुन्छ। मानिसको शरीरभित्र पर्ने जुका (Ascaris) लगायत यस समूहमा पर्ने केही जीवहरू परजीवी हुन्।

subject: science
en_record_text: They are unisexual i.e. the male and the female sexes are separate. The male is shorter and smaller than the female. Reproduction takes place internally.
ne_record_text: यिनीहरूमा भाले र पोथी छुट्टिएका हुन्छन्। पोथीको तुलनामा भाले छोटो र सानो हुन्छ। यिनीहरूको मेलद्वारा नै प्रजनन हुन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: They have a food pipe in their body that joins their mouth with their anus. Most of the organisms in this group live in soil and water. Other organisms like leech live as parasites and suck the blood of other animals.
ne_record_text: यिनीहरूको शरीरमा मुखदेखि मलद्वारसम्म फैलने गरी खाना नली हुन्छ। यिनीहरूमध्ये केही पानी र माटोमा हुर्कन्छन् भने जुगा जस्तो जीव अन्य प्राणीको रगत चुसेर परजीवीको रूपमा बाँच्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text:
Annelida 
 The organisms that belong to this group have a long, segmented and cylindrical body. Their body is covered by soft and moist skin. 
 They have a food pipe in their body that joins their mouth with their anus. Most of the organisms in this group live in soil and water. Others like leech live as parasites and suck the blood of other animals. 
 Many creatures of this group breathe through skin and some breathe through gills. 
 They have blood vessels in their body and they also have a simple nervous system.
ne_record_text:
एनेलिडा (Annelida) 
 यी जीवहरूको शरीर खण्ड खण्ड परेको, लामो र गोलाकार हुने भएकोले यिनीहरूलाई एनेलिडा भनिन्छ। यिनीहरूको शरीर नरम र रसिलो छालाले ढाकेको हुन्छ। 
 यिनीहरूको शरीरमा मुखदेखि मलद्वारसम्म फैलने गरी लामो खाना नली हुन्छ। यिनीहरूमध्ये केही पानी र माटोमा हुर्कन्छन् भने जुगा जस्तो जीव अन्य प्राणीको रगत चुसेर परजीवीको रूपमा बाँच्छ। 
 यिनीहरूमध्ये धेरैले शरीरको बाहिरी सतहबाट सास फेर्छन् भने केहीले साधारण किसिमको फुल्का (Gills) को प्रयोग गर्छन्। 
 यिनीहरूको शरीरमा रक्तनली हुने भएकोले रक्तसञ्चार हुनुका साथै साधारण किसिमको मष्तिष्क प्रणालीको समेत विकास भएको हुन्छ।

 Many creatures of this group breathe through skin and some breathe through gills. 
 They have blood vessels in their body and they also have a simple nervous system.
ne_record_text:
एनेलिडा (Annelida) 
 यी जीवहरूको शरीर खण्ड खण्ड परेको, लामो र गोलाकार हुने भएकोले यिनीहरूलाई एनेलिडा भनिन्छ। यिनीहरूको शरीर नरम र रसिलो छालाले ढाकेको हुन्छ। 
 यिनीहरूको शरीरमा मुखदेखि मलद्वारसम्म फैलने गरी लामो खाना नली हुन्छ। यिनीहरूमध्ये केही पानी र माटोमा हुर्कन्छन् भने जुगा जस्तो जीव अन्य प्राणीको रगत चुसेर परजीवीको रूपमा बाँच्छ। 
 यिनीहरूमध्ये धेरैले शरीरको बाहिरी सतहबाट सास फेर्छन् भने केहीले साधारण किसिमको फुल्का (Gills) को प्रयोग गर्छन्। 
 यिनीहरूको शरीरमा रक्तनली हुने भएकोले रक्तसञ्चार हुनुका साथै साधारण किसिमको मष्तिष्क प्रणालीको समेत विकास भएको हुन्छ।

ne_record_text: काण्डले धेरै काम गर्छ। यसले पात, हाँगा फल र फूललाई अड्याउनुका साथै जरासँग जोड्ने काम पनि गर्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: #Ovary@ is the part of the plant which lies usually at the bottom of the flower. It contains ovules. It has the seeds inside and turns into the fruit that we eat.
ne_record_text: सामान्यतया फूलको तल्लो भागमा #गर्भाशय (Ovary)@ हुन्छ। गर्भाशयभित्र ओभ्युल्स हुन्छ। यसबाट फल बन्छ। फलभित्र बिउ सुरक्षित रहन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: The little leaves that are underneath the flower are called the #calyx@. They protect the bud. Single part of the #calyx@ is known as sepal. Some flowers have only few sepals, while others have many.
ne_record_text: फूलको तल्लो भागमा रहेको स-साना पातहरूलाई #पत्रदल@ भनिन्छ। यसले कोपिला अवस्थामा सुरक्षा दिने काम गर्छ। कुनै बिरुवामा थोरै पत्रदल हुन्छन् भने कुनैमा धेरै हुन सक्छन्।

subject: science
en_record_text: Light is a form of energy. The objects that have their own light is known as luminous objects whereas those that do not have their own light are known as non-luminous objects. Based on whether light can pass through them or not, objects can be transparent, translucent or opaque. Light always travel in a straight line.The phenomenon of returning of the light in the same medium after striking a surface is called reflection of light. We will learn more about these in this lesson.
ne_record_text: प्रकाश शक्तिको रूप हो। आफ्नो प्रकाश भएका वस्तुलाई दिप्त भनेजस्तै प्रकाश नहुने वस्तुलाई अदिप्त वस्तु भनिन्छ। प्रकाश छिर्ने वा नछिर्ने गरी वस्तुहरूलाई पारदर्शक, अर्धपारदर्शक र अपारदर्शक हुन्छन्। प्रकाश सधैँ सरल रेखामा जान्छ। कुनै सतहमा ठोकिएको प्रकाश फर्केर आउने प्रक्रियालाई प्रकाश परावर्तन भनिन्छ। यस पाठमा यी विषयहरुबारे अझै धेरै जान्नेछौँ।

पाठको शिर्षकभित्र छिर्ने बित्तिकै तपाईले पाठ्यक्रम निर्देशित उदेश्य देख्नुहुनेछ। त्यसको तलपट्टि पाठभित्र के छ भन्नेर कुराको सामान्य जानकारी पाउन सक्नुहुन्छ। अब ‘अगाडि बढ’ मा क्लिक गरी पाठभित्र छिर्नुहोस्।प्रकाश शक्तिको बारेमा जान्नपको लागि परिचयमा क्लिक गर्नुहोस् (अथवा आफूलाई मनलागेको शिर्षक पनि छान्न सक्नुहुनेछ)। अर्को पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। यहाँ स्क्रिनमा दिप्त र अदिप्त वस्तुहरू देखाइएका छन्। सुरू गर भन्ने बटनमा क्लिक गरी सहि उत्तर छान्नु होस्। प्रत्येक नयाँ वस्तुको लागि Sequence control button दबाउनुहोस्।फेरि खेल्न सङ्केतमा क्लिक गर्नुहोस्। अब नयाँ पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। टर्च लाईटको बाटोमा पर्ने गरी तलका वस्तुहरूलाई पालैपालो राख्न प्रत्येकमा क्लिक गरी हेर्नुहोस्। X मा क्लिक गरी फर्कनुहोस्। अब तपाई पृष्ठ नम्बर पाँचमा जानुहोस्। यहाँ देखिएका चित्रहरूमा पालैपालो क्लिक गरी ती वस्तुले बनाउने छायाँ हेर्नुहोस्। उक्त छायाँलाई ठुलो वा सानो बनाउनको लागि टर्चमा क्लिक गर्नुहोस्। अर्को पृष्ठमा जानुहोस् र कुनै एक छान भनेको मध्येबाट एउटा विकल्पमा क्लिक गर्नुहोस् अथवा पालैपालो क्लिक गरेर हेर्नुहोस्। अब सातौँ पृष्ठमा क्लिक गर्नुहोस्। यहाँसम्म तपाई ‘प्रकाश’ उपशिर्षक अन्तर्गत हुनुहुन्छ। 
 अब ‘प्रकाशको परावर्तन’ मा क्लिक गर्नुहोस्। स्क्रिनमा देखिएको प्रश्न राम्ररी पढेर त्यसको बारेमा सोच्नुहोस् र उत्तशर लेखेको ठाउँमा क्लिक गरी तपाईको आफ्नै उत्तरर जाँच्नुहोस्। X मा क्लिक, गरेर फर्कनुहोस्। प्रकाश परावर्तन हुने केही नियमहरू जान्नफको लागि अर्को पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। फेरि अर्को पृष्ठमा गएर केलिडोस्कोपको बारेमा जान्नुहहोस्। यहाँ तपाई आफैँँले केलिडोस्कोप बनाउन सिक्नुहुनेछ। त्यसको लागि अर्को पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। आवश्यक परेमा सङ्केतमा क्लिक गरी पुन: क्रियाकलाप दोहोर्यााउनुहोस्। अन्यथा Sequence control button मा क्लिक गर्दै चरणबद्द रूपमा केलिडोस्कोप बनाउन सिक्नुहोस्। अब पृष्ठ नम्बर ५ मा पुगेर पेरिस्कोपको बारेमा जान्नु्होस्। यहाँ तपाई आफैँँले पेरिस्कोप बनाउन सिक्नुहुनेछ। त्यसको लागि अर्को पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। आवश्यक परेमा सङ्केतमा क्लिक गरी पुन: क्रियाकलाप दोहोर्याीउनुहोस्। अन्यथा Sequence control button मा क्लिक गर्दै चरणबद्द रूपमा पेरिस्कोप बनाउन सिक्नुहोस्। पेरिस्कोपमा आकृति कसरी देखिन्छ? भन्नेप कुरा थाहा पाउनको लागि अन्तिम पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। Sequence control button मा क्लिक गर्नुहोस् र ‘फेरि खेलौँ’ मा क्लिक गरी क्रियाकलाप दोहोर्या उनुहोस्।

subject: science
en_record_text: Divide the children into 3/4 groups as per the class size. Provide them some objects and their shadows. Let the children match all objects with their respective shadows. If Possible let them create different shadows with the help of their hands. Please help them be more engaged.
ne_record_text: विद्यार्थीहरूलाई तीन वा चार समूहमा विभाजन गर्नुहोस्। विभिन्न वस्तुहरू दिएर तिनले बनाउने छायाँ देखाई ‘त्यो कुन वस्तुको छायाँ हो’ भनी विद्यार्थीलाई सोध्नुहोस्। विद्यार्थीको सहभागितामा अभिबृद्दि गर्न सहयोग गर्नुहोस्।

subject: science
en_record_text: Show some objects to the children and ask if those objects are transparent, semi-transparent or opaque. Also divide the children ito several groups and ask them to make a model of periscope, kaleidoscope etc. of different shape and size. For this, provide or arrange all the required materials. Finally, ask them to present their project.
ne_record_text: विद्यार्थीहरूलाई आवश्यकता अनुसार फरक फरक वस्तुहरू देखाई ती वस्तुहरू पारदर्शी, अर्धपारदर्शी वा अपारदर्शी के हो, सोध्नुहोस्। विद्यार्थीहरूलाई विभाजन गरी प्रकाश परावर्तन गर्ने उपकरणहरू जस्तै; पेरिस्कोप, केलिडोस्कोप आदि बनाउन लगाउनुहोस्। साथै तिनका कार्य पद्दतिबारे कक्षामा प्रस्तुतिकरण दिन लगाउनुहोस्।

subject: science
en_record_text: Before get started; close all the windows and doors of the classroom. Now, exchange the position of bags of the children and let them tell about the color and shape of the bag in front of them without touching it. Again repeat the same question after opening doors and windows. Ask them if their answers differ in absence and presence of light. Discuss on the reason behind it. Then after, show some objects or write on the board so that they could identify whether an object has its own light or not i.e. luminous or non-luminous.
ne_record_text: पाठ पढाउनु अगाडि सम्भव भएसम्म कक्षामा भएका सबै झ्याल र ढोकाहरू बन्द गर्नुहोस्। अब विद्यार्थीहरूको झोला फेरबदल गरी दिनुहोस् र उनीहरूलाई आफू अगाडिको झोला कस्तो रङ्ग र आकारको छ भन्ने जस्ता प्रश्नहरू सोध्नुहोस्। अब झ्याल र ढोकाहरू खोलेपछि पुन: त्यहि प्रश्न सोध्नुहोस्। किन त्यसो भएको होला भनेर छलफल गराउनुहोस्। त्यसपछि कुन कुन वस्तुहरूको आफ्नै प्रकाश हुन्छ वा हुँदैन भनेर सोध्नुहोस्।

 अब ‘प्रकाशको परावर्तन’ मा क्लिक गर्नुहोस्। स्क्रिनमा देखिएको प्रश्न राम्ररी पढेर त्यसको बारेमा सोच्नुहोस् र उत्तर लेखेको ठाउँमा क्लिक गरी तपाईको आफ्नै उत्तर जाँच्नुहोस्। X मा क्लिक, गरेर फर्कनुहोस्। प्रकाश परावर्तन हुने केही नियमहरू जान्नको लागि अर्को पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। फेरि अर्को पृष्ठमा गएर केलिडोस्कोपको बारेमा जान्नुहोस्। यहाँ तपाई आफैँँले केलिडोस्कोप बनाउन सिक्नुहुनेछ। त्यसको लागि अर्को पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। आवश्यक परेमा सङ्केतमा क्लिक गरी पुन: क्रियाकलाप दोहोर्याउनुहोस्। अन्यथा Sequence control button मा क्लिक गर्दै चरणबद्द रूपमा केलिडोस्कोप बनाउन सिक्नुहोस्। अब पृष्ठ नम्बर ५ मा पुगेर पेरिस्कोपको बारेमा जान्नुहोस्। यहाँ तपाई आफैँँले पेरिस्कोप बनाउन सिक्नुहुनेछ। त्यसको लागि अर्को पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। आवश्यक परेमा सङ्केतमा क्लिक गरी पुन: क्रियाकलाप दोहोर्याउनुहोस्। अन्यथा Sequence control button मा क्लिक गर्दै चरणबद्द रूपमा पेरिस्कोप बनाउन सिक्नुहोस्। पेरिस्कोपमा आकृति कसरी देखिन्छ? भन्ने कुरा थाहा पाउनको लागि अन्तिम पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। Sequence control button मा क्लिक गर्नुहोस् र ‘फेरि खेलौँ’ मा क्लिक गरी क्रियाकलाप दोहोर्याउनुहोस्।

Start with the name of set, which is always in capital letters. Then write the variable within curly brackets.

समूहको नामबाट सुरु गर्नुपर्छ। यो जहिले पनि अङ्ग्रेजी ठूला अक्षरमा लेखिन्छ। त्यसपछि, मझौला कोष्ठहरू ‘{ }’भित्र चल लेख्नुपर्छ। 
 अनि #":"@ चिह्न लेख्नुपर्छ। यो चिह्नलाई 'भनेको' भनेर पढिन्छ भने यसले चललाई परिभाषित गर्दछ। 
 अन्त्यमा समूहका सदस्यहरूको साझा गुणका आधारमा व्याख्या लेख्नुपर्छ।

Interest of Rs. 100 in 1 year = Rs. 10 
 Interest of Re. 1 in 1 year = Rs. 10/100 
 Interest of Rs. 120 in 12 months = Rs. 10/100 × 120 = Rs. 12

 Then, see how much you have to pay for the total money.

यो तपाईँले ऐकिक नियमको प्रयोग गरेर निकाल्न सक्नु हुन्छ।

 रु. 1 को लागि 1 वर्षमा, कति तिर्नु पर्छ पत्ता लगाउनुहोस्।
 त्यसपछि, पुरै पैसाको लागि कति तिर्नु पर्छ हेर्नुहोस्।
 पर्खनु म यसको अझै वर्णन गर्छु।

    For Re. 1, you have to pay Rs. 10/100 after 1 year.
 3.Use unitary method to find the interest for Rs. 20,000 in 1 year:
    For Rs. 20000, you have to pay Rs. 10/100 × 20000 in 1 year.

subject: math
en_record_text:
Always start with the interest rate.
 Interest rate is R%. That is:
        For Rs. 100, the interest is R in 1 year.
 Let’s use unitary method for interest rate.
         For Re. 1, the interest is ::R_/_100;; in 1 year.
 The interest for Rs. P is ::R_/_100;; × P = ::PR_/_100;; in 1 year.
 The interest rate for Rs. P = ::PR_/_100;; × T in T years = ::PRT_/_100;;
ne_record_text:
जहिले पनि ब्याजदरबाट सुरु गर।
 ब्याजदर R% छ। यसको मतलब:
        रु. 100 को 1 वर्षको ब्याज = रु. R हुन्छ।
 ब्याजदरको लागि ऐकिक नियमको प्रयोग गरौँ।
         रु. 1 को 1 वर्षको ब्याज = रु. ::R_/_100;; हुन्छ।
 रु. P को 1 वर्षको ब्याज = रु. ::R_/_100;; X P = ::PR_/_100;; हुन्छ।
 रु. P को T वर्षको ब्याज = रु. ::PR_/_100;; X T = रु. ::PRT_/_100;; हुन्छ।

That is fine. If the time is given in months, we again use unitary method.

 Let’s find how.
 We know that:
        Interest rate in 1 year is Rs. 400
 Since 1 year = 12 months, we can also write:
 Interest rate in 12 months is Rs. 400
 Interest rate in 1 month is Rs. ::400_/_12;; (UNITARY METHOD)
 Interest rate in 8 months is Rs. ::400_/_12;; × 8 = Rs.266.67

यदि समय बर्षको सट्टा महिनामा छ भने, हामी फेरी ऐकिक नियमको प्रयोग गर्छौँ।

 कसरी निकाल्ने त? आऊ हेरौँ।
 हामीलाई थाहा छ

 1 वर्ष = 12 महिना हुन्छ, त्यसैले,
 12 महिनाको ब्याज रु. 400 हो पनि भन्न सक्छौँ
 1 महिनाको ब्याज रु. ::400_/_12;; हुन्छ (ऐेकिक नियम)
 8 महिनाको ब्याज रु. ::400_/_12;; X 8 = रु. 266.67 हुन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: Soil is filtered out from the water. However, the water is not drinkable until it is boiled. After the water is boiled for at least three minutes, you can drink the water upon cooling.

subject: science
en_record_text: When the magnet is brought near the mixture, it attracts pieces of iron and separates them from rice grains.

subject: science
en_record_text: This chapter will provide information on chemical that are useful in our daily life such as phenol,dettol,detergent,baking powder and chemical fertilizer etc. In addition, the application of these chemicals are also explained.
ne_record_text:
यस पाठमा दैनिक जीवनमा उपयोगी हुने केही रसायनहरूको बारेमा महत्वपूर्ण जानकारी हासिल गर्न सकिने छ। जस्तै: फेनोल, डेटोल, डिटरजेन्ट, बेकिङ पाउडर र रासायनिक मल आदि। यसका साथै यी रसायनहरूको उपयोगीताहरू पनि बताइने छ। यस पाठमा तिमीले यी
 विषयहरूबारे अझै धेरै जान्नेछौ।

subject: science

subject: science
en_record_text: Some chemicals are artificially created in industries like phenol, dettol, detergents, baking powder and chemical fertilizer.
ne_record_text: केही रसायनहरू औद्योगिक रूपमा पनि बनाइन्छन्। जस्तै: फेनोल, डेटोल, डिटरजेन्ट, बेकिङ पाउडर, रासायनिक मल आदि।

subject: science
en_record_text: Phenol is a colorless or white crystalline solid organic compound,smelling a disinfectant. The molecular formula for phenol is C 6 H 5 OH, this means it is industrial product compose of carbon, hydrogen and oxygen. The source of phenol are plants and small animals.
ne_record_text:
फेनोल एक गन्धयुक्त यौगीक हो जुन रङ्गहीन हुन्छ। यो पानीमा सजिलै घुल्छ। यसको आणविक सूत्र C 6 H 5 OH हो।
 यसको मतलब फेनोल कार्बन, हाईड्रोजन र अक्सिजनलाई मिलाएर बनाइएको औद्योगिक उत्पादन हो। फेनोलको स्रोत भनेकै बिरूवा र स-साना जीवहरू हुन्।

subject: science
en_record_text: Dettol is product related to sanitation. Its molecular formula is C8H9ClO which means dettol is made up of carbon, hydrogen, chlorine and oxygen.
ne_record_text: डेटोल सरसफाइसँग सम्बन्धित एउटा उत्पादन हो। यो एक किसिमको प्रतिस्थापित फेनोल नै हो। यसको आणविक सूत्र C8H9ClO हो। अर्थात् कार्बन, हाइड्रोजन, क्लोरिन र अक्सिजनलाई मिलाएर डेटोल बनाइन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: Detergent is a cleansing substance like soap but, unlike soap, it is made up of chemical compounds rather than fats and lye and allows all grease and dirt to be carried away.
ne_record_text: डिटरजेन्ट साबुनजस्तै सफाइ गर्ने रसायन हो। यसले वस्तु (जस्तै कपडा) मा भएको चिल्लो र मैलो निकाल्ने काम गर्छ। डिटरजेन्टलाई साबुनमा झैँ बोसो नराखी बनाइन्छ। बरू यसलाई रासायनिक पदार्थको प्रयोग गरी औद्योगिक रूपमा बनाइन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: Baking powder is a chemical used as a raising agent for cakes, biscuits and breads. It is white color powder. When baking powder is mixed with dough and warmed , chemical reaction will produces carbon dioxide and it will increase volume of baked goods.
ne_record_text:
बेकिङ पाउडर केक, बिस्कुट र पाउरोटी आदिलाई राम्ररी उस्काउन प्रयोग गरिने एक किसिमको रसायन हो। अर्थात् यसले पिठोबाट बन्ने केही परिकारहरूको आयतन वृद्दि गर्छ। यो सेतो रङ्गको धुलो हो। मुछेको पिठोमा बेकिङ पाउडरलाई मिसाएर ओभन
 वा तावामा तताउँदा रासायनिक प्रतिक्रिया भई त्यसबाट कार्बनडाइअक्साइड बाहिरिन्छ। जसको फलस्वरूप पिठो फुलेर उस्कन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: Chemical fertilizer is also called artificial fertilizers. The chemical fertilizer uses different kinds of elements like nitrogen, potassium, phosphorous, petroleum etc to manufacture it in industry. It is use to increase production.
ne_record_text:
रासायनिक मललाई कृत्रिम मल पनि भनिन्छ। विभिन्न किसिमका तत्वहरू जस्तै: नाइट्रोजन, पोटासियम, फोस्फोरस, पेट्रोलियम पदार्थ आदि मिलाएर औद्योगिक रूपमा रासायनिक मल बनाइन्छ। छोटो समयमा धेरै परिमाणको बाली उब्जाउनको लागि
 उक्त मल माटोमा छरिन्छ। सामान्यतया रासायनिक मललाई गेडा मलको रूपमा पनि चिनिन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: Phenol and its vapor is harmful.so,keep out of reach of children.
ne_record_text: फेनोल र यसको बाफ हानिकारक हुन्छ। त्यसैले यसलाई स-साना बालबालिकाको पहुँच भन्दा टाढा राख्नु सुरक्षित हुनेछ।

subject: science
en_record_text: It is directly disposed in streams which will adversely affect aquatic life.
ne_record_text: शहरी क्षेत्रमा सीधै यसलाई खोलामा मिसाइन्छ। त्यसैको कारणले गर्दा पानीमा फिँजहरू निस्कन्छन्, जसको प्रत्यक्ष प्रभाव जलप्राणीमा पर्न जान्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: In this lesson you will learn about earth and universe. You will be able to recognize sun as star and solar system as a combination of sun and planets orbiting the sun . You will be able to differentiate between planets and stars and recognize constellation.
ne_record_text: यस पाठमा तिमीले पृथ्वी र अन्तरिक्षको बारेमा जान्नेछौ। सूर्य एक तारा हो र यसको वरिपरि घुम्ने ग्रहहरू मिलेर सौर्यमण्डल बनेको हुन्छ भन्ने कुरा पनि पत्ता लगाउने छौे। साथै तारा र ग्रहबिचमा पाइने भिन्नताका बारेमा जानकारी पाउनुको साथै ताराहरूको समूह मिलेर नै तारामण्डल बनेको हुन्छ भन्ने तथ्य पत्ता लगाउने छौ। यस पाठमा तिमीले अझै धेरै सिक्नेछौ।

subject: science
en_record_text: I am the third planet from the sun. Who am I?
ne_record_text: सूर्यबाट म तेस्रो स्थानमा पर्दछु, भन म को हुँ?

subject: science
en_record_text: A constellation is a group of stars, which seem to be forming different shapes at night. Imaginary shapes like that of animals can be seen among these group of stars. There are 88 officially recognized constellations and among them 12 are horoscopes.
ne_record_text: रातको सफा आकाशमा ताराहरूको विभिन्न निश्चित आकारमा देखिने समूहलाइ तारामण्डल भनिन्छ। ती तारामण्डललाई राम्ररी हेरेमा विभिन्न किसिमको काल्पनिक वस्तु, जनावर आदिको आकृति देखिन्छ। अहिलेसम्म जम्मा 88 ओटा तारामण्डलहरू पत्ता लागिसकेका छन्। जसमध्ये 12 ओटालाई राशि भनिन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: For thousands of years humans were unaware of the solar system and believed that earth was at the center of the universe.
ne_record_text: हजारौँ वर्षसम्म पनि मानिसहरूलाई सौर्यमण्डलबारे थाहा थिएन। त्यसैले त्यतिबेला पृथ्वी अन्तरिक्षको केन्द्रमा नै छ भन्ने विश्वास गरिन्थ्यो।

subject: science
en_record_text:
Besides the twinkling stars, you may also see another kind of luminous bodies : planets
Earth where we live on is also a planet. You will be surprised to know that with earth we are also located in sky.
ne_record_text: आकाशमा ताराहरू बाहेक पनि थुप्रै ठूला ठूला पिण्डहरू हुन्छन्। जस्तै: ग्रह। हामी बस्ने पृथ्वी पनि एउटा ग्रह नै हो। तिमीलाई अचम्म लाग्ला, तर पृथ्वीसँगै हामी पनि आकाश मै छौँ नि!

subject: science
en_record_text: Yes, moon. Although Moon, which rotates around earth, is smaller than other planets , it appears biggest and brightest entity in the sky. It appears so because it is nearest one to our earth. Moon doesn’t glow on its own , but only reflects light from sun like mirror.
ne_record_text: पृथ्वीको वरिपरि घुम्ने चन्द्रमा ग्रह भन्दा सानो भएपनि नजिक भएकोले ठुलो र चम्किलो देखिन्छ। आफ्नो प्रकाश नभएपनि ऐनामा जस्तै गरी सूर्यको प्रकाश परेपछि यो चम्कन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: In which night moon shines bright? Think. Ask your friend too.
ne_record_text: ल भन त कुन रातमा तिमीले चन्द्रमालाई सबैभन्दा चम्किलो देख्छौ? साथीलाई पनि सोध है।

subject: science
en_record_text:
You might be curious to know about these different bright things on the sky.
How big are they? How far are they? How old are they?
ne_record_text: यी सबै चम्किला वस्तुहरू देख्दा पक्कै तिमीलाई जिज्ञासा लागेको होला की तिनीहरू कत्रा होलान्? कति टाढा होलान्? अनि कति पुराना होलान्?

subject: science
en_record_text: Among moon, sun, stars and planets you may know much about sun and moon.
ne_record_text: यी त भए चन्द्रमा, सूर्य, तारा र ग्रहहरू। जसमध्ये चन्द्रमा र सूर्यको बारेमा त तिमीलाई धेरै कुरा थाहा होला।

subject: science
en_record_text:
The stars appear as blazing ball of fire that are very massive (40 km to 900,000,000 km) and heavy that the pressure inside them creates heat and light.
Like sun.
ne_record_text: ताराहरू बलिरहेका बल जस्तै देखिने अति ठूला (40 km to 900,000,000 km) र असाध्यै गह्रौँ पिण्ड हुन्। यसको कारण चाप पैदा भई अत्यधिक ताप र प्रकाश निस्कन्छ। जस्तै: सूर्य।

subject: science
en_record_text: Since the stars are very far away, the telescope can only make them look brighter but not larger.
ne_record_text: टेलिस्कोपबाट हेर्दा ताराहरू अझै चम्किला देखिए पनि आकारमा भने ठूला देखिँदैनन्। यसको एउटै कारण के हो भने पृथ्वीबाट ताराहरू धेरै नै टाढा छन्।

subject: science
en_record_text: Compare to massive stars planets are very small (2,440 km to 69,911 km). Unlike stars planets do not have heat and light.
ne_record_text:
ठूला ताराको अनुपातमा ग्रहहरू निकै साना (2,440 km to 69,911 km) हुन्छन्। ताराको जस्तो ग्रहको आफ्नो ताप र प्रकाश हुँदैन।
जस्तै: पृथ्वी।

subject: science
en_record_text: Sun is a star. Earth is sun’s planet.
ne_record_text: सूर्य हाम्रो तारा हो भने पृथ्वी यसको ग्रह हो।

subject: science
en_record_text: Can you guess how does the sun appear on other planets?
ne_record_text: पृथ्वी बाहेकका अन्य ग्रहहरूबाट चाहिँ सूर्यलाई हेर्दा कस्तो देखिन्छ होला? हेरौँ।

subject: science
en_record_text: This is what Sun looks like from Mars , a little planet next to earth.
ne_record_text: यो पृथ्वी नजिकैको मङ्गल ग्रहबाट देखिने सूर्यको तस्बिर हो। त्यहाँबाट पनि सूर्यलाई उस्तै देखिने रहेछ।

subject: science
en_record_text: There are other eight members of the solar system except the sun which are called planets. They are Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn , Uranus and Neptune. Sun is located at the center of solar system. The gravitational pull of sun keeps planets in their orbit and make them rotate around it. In same way planets are orbited by their moon (satellite). The organised family of sun, moon and planets is called solar system.
ne_record_text: सूर्यबाहेक यस सौर्यमण्डलमा अन्य आठओटा सदस्यहरू छन्, जसलाई ग्रह भनिन्छ। ति हुन् : बुध (Mercury), शुक्र (Venus), पृथ्वी (Earth), मङ्गल (Mars), वृहस्पति (Jupiter), शनि (Saturn), अरुण (Uranus) र बरुण (Neptune)। सूर्य केन्द्रमा स्थिर रहन्छ भने सूर्यको अत्यधिक गुरुत्वाकर्षण शक्तिको कारणले गर्दा यी सबै ग्रहहरूले सूर्यको परिक्रमा गर्दछन्। त्यसरी नै यी ग्रहहरूलाई चाहिँ तिनका चन्द्रमा (उपग्रह) ले परिक्रमा गर्दछन्। यसरी सूर्य, ग्रह र चन्द्रमाको सङ्गठित परिवारलाई नै सौर्यमण्डल भनिन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: Venus is brightest planet.Venus is so bright because its thick clouds reflect most of the sunlight that reaches it (about 70%) back into space, and because it is the closest planet to Earth.
ne_record_text: पृथ्वीबाट हेर्दा शुक्र नै सबैभन्दा चम्किलो ग्रह हो। शुक्रलाई बादलले वरिपरिबाट घेरेको छ। यसले गर्दा सूर्यको प्रकाश परावर्तित भएर पृथ्वीमा आइपुग्छ। फलस्वरूप शुक्रलाई चम्किलो देखिन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: Mercury is the smallest and closest to the Sun of the eight planets in the Solar System.
ne_record_text: सौर्यमण्डलमा भएका आठओटा ग्रहहरू मध्ये बुध (Mercury) सबैभन्दा सानो र सूर्यबाट नजिकको ग्रह हो। यसको बारेमा केही बुँदाहरू हेरौँ।

subject: science
en_record_text: Venus is the second planet from the Sun. It is named after the Roman goddess of love and beauty. In size and mass, it is similar to Earth, and is often described as Earth's "sister" or "twin".
ne_record_text: शुक्र (Venus) ग्रह सूर्यबाट दोस्रो स्थानमा पर्दछ। रोमनहरूले यसलाई ‘माया र सौन्दर्यको’ प्रतिक मान्दछन्। पृथ्वीको नाप र पिण्ड यस ग्रहसँग लगभग बराबर भएकोले यी दुई ग्रहहरूलाई ‘जुम्ल्याहा’ पनि भनिन्छ। यसको बारेमा केही बुँदाहरू हेरौँ।

subject: science
en_record_text: Earth is the third planet from the Sun which accommodate life. Human, animals, birds, insects and plants have made their home in earth.

en_record_text: Look! Now all sugar gets dissolved. It means still more sugar can be dissolved as in unsaturated solution. It happens because the capacity of solvent to dissolve solute increases as temperature of the solution rises.
ne_record_text: अघि घुल्न नसकेको चिनी अबभने घुल्यो। यहाँ असन्तृप्त घोलमा जस्तै थप चिनी घुल्न सक्ने अवस्था देखा पर्यो। यसो हुनुको कारण के हो भने तापक्रम बढाउँदा घोलको थप घुलित पदार्थ घुलाउन सक्ने क्षमतामा पनि वृद्दि हुन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: At any temperature, a liquid substance (called the solvent) has a capacity to dissolve certain amount of a substance (called the solute) is known as solubility. But solubility increases with the rise in temperature. It means, more solute can be dissolved in the solution. Now let’s learn about saturated solution.
ne_record_text: कुनै पनि तापक्रममा घोल (जस्तै: पानी) ले घुलित पदार्थ (जस्तै: चिनी) लाई घुलाउन सक्ने क्षमता निश्चत हुन्छ। यसलाई घोलको घोल्यमात्रा (Solubility) भनिन्छ। तर तापक्रम बढाउँदा घोल्यमात्रामा पनि वृद्दि हुन्छ। अर्थात्, घोलमा थप घुलित पदार्थ घुल्न सक्छ। त्यसो भए सन्तृप्त घोल कस्तो हुन्छ, हेरौँ।

ne_record_text: हाम्रो व्यवहारिक जीवनमा प्रयोग हुने विभिन्न किसिमका सरल यन्त्रहरू सङ्कलन गर्नुहोस् (असम्भव भए कागजको चिटमा सरल यन्त्रहरूको नाम लेख्न लगाउनुहोस्।)। जस्तै: चम्चा, किला, फल्याक, गल, चक्कु स्टेप्लर, घिर्नी आदि। ती सबै वस्तुहरूलाई एकै ठाउँमा राख्नुहोस् र विद्यार्थीहरूलाई ६ ओटा समूहमा विभाजन गरी तिनीहरूलाई उत्तोलक, पेच, पाङ्ग्रा र बीँड, घिर्नी, छड्के सतह र फेसोको नाम दिनुहोस्। अब तिनीहरूलाई आफ्नो समूह अन्तर्गतको मात्र सरल यन्त्र सङ्कलन गर्न लगाउनुहोस्। अन्तमा हरेक समूहलाई उक्त सरल यन्त्रको उपयोगिता भन्न लगाउनुहोस्।

en_record_text: Ask the children some questions so that they would pay their attention towards the utility of simple machines.Divide the students into two different groups and provide them two magnets to each group. Now ask them try out to join either poles to each other. Tell them to share their experience while joining opposite poles if they have any. Then provide some objects so that they could easily find magnetic ones. If possible, also provide them some iron filings and tell them to keep a magnet where iron filings are scattered. Through this activity, get them aware of magnetic lines of force.
ne_record_text: विद्यार्थीहरूलाई कुन सरल यन्त्रको प्रयोग के कामको लागि गरिन्छ भन्ने कुराको बारेमा ध्यानाकर्षण गराउनको लागि यस्ता खालका प्रश्नहरू सोध्नुहोस्। जस्तै: सुचीकारले कपडा के ले काट्छ? हात नपस्ने सिसीभित्रको ध्यू केले झिक्न सजिलो हुन्छ? आगो बाल्दा गोल केले समात्छौ? तरकारी केले काट्छौ? आदि। उनीहरूको उत्तर कालो/सेतो पाटीमा टिपेर यी सबै सरल यन्त्र हुन् जसको उपयोगीता व्यापक छ भन्ने कुरा उनीहरुलाई बुझाई दिनुहोस्। साथै यसलाई ६ भागमा विभाजन समेत गरिएको छ र ती केके हुन् भन्ने कुराको जानकारी समेत गराई दिनुहोस्।

A war (1939–45) in which the Axis Powers (Germany, Italy, and Japan) were defeated by an alliance eventually including the United Kingdom and its dominions, the Soviet Union, and the United States.
 Hitler's invasion of Poland in September 1939 led Great Britain and France to declare war on Germany. Germany defeated and occupied France the following year and soon overran much of Europe. Italy joined the war in 1940, and the US and Japan entered following the Japanese attack on the US fleet at Pearl Harbor. Italy surrendered in 1943 and the Allies launched a full-scale invasion in Normandy in June 1944. The war in Europe ended when Germany surrendered in May 1945; Japan surrendered after the US dropped atom bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki in August 1945. An estimated 55 million people were killed during the war, including a much higher proportion of civilians than in the First World War.

en_record_text:
It is 1942 in Holland. It is the time of the Second World War, and the Germans have invaded the country.
 All the Jews are frightened for their lives. The Frank family decides to hide in a secret flat in Mr. Frank's
 office building. Their younger daughter, Anne, begins to write a diary of their lives in hiding. 
 
 Soon another Jewish family, Mr. and Mrs. Van Daan and their son Peter; join the Franks. Life in the hiding-place
 is full of arguments and dangers, and the people are often hungry and frightened. But they hope for the best too,
 until that final day when the police arrive ... 
 
 Although this book is like an adventure story, it is all true. Young Anne Frank wrote her diary in the Secret Annexe, the family's hiding-place in Amsterdam. 
 After the war, her father wanted the world to know about the diary. Anne herself was no longer alive then; the Germans took her away to a concentration camp with her sister, Margot. She died there in 1945, just before the British soldiers arrived to save the prisoners. 
 Anne's diary tells us about the awful suffering of the Jews during the war. But it is also a story about love and hope for the future. 
 Anne Frank was born on 12 June 1929 in Frankfurt-am-Main, Germany. She died early 1945 in the Bergen-Belsen concentration camp near Hanover, Germany.

subject: english
en_record_text:
On the morning of 4 August 1944, a car arrived at 263 Prinsengracht, the address of the Secret Annexe. German and Dutch police arrested the eight people who were hiding in the Annexe. Somebody must have told the authorities that they were hiding there. 
 Margot and Anne Frank were taken from Auschwitz to the Bergen- Belsen concentration camp near Hanover, in Germany. A terrible illness attacked the prisoners there. They both died in the winter of 1944-5. Anne must have died in late February or early March. All the bodies of the prisoners were thrown together. The British army arrived in the camp on 12 April 1945. 
 Otto Frank was the only one of the eight still alive. After Russian soldiers reached Auschwitz, he was finally taken back to Amsterdam. In 1953, he moved to Switzerland, married again, and lived there until his death in 1980. He spent the rest of his life trying to share the message of his daughter's diary with the rest of the world.

On Friday, June 12th, I woke up early at six o'clock; it was my birthday. I'm not allowed to get up then, so I had to wait until quarter
 to seven. Then I went down to the dining room, where Moortje, my cat, welcomed me. At seven I went in to meet Mummy and Daddy, and then 
 to the sitting room for my presents. The nicest present was you — my diary! There was a bunch of roses on the table, and lots more flowers 
 and presents arrived for me during the day. Daddy and Mummy gave me a blue blouse, a game and a bottle of 
 fruit juice which tastes quite like wine!
 At school, I shared some cakes with my friends, and I was allowed to choose the game that we played in the sports lesson. Afterwards, all my 
 friends danced round me in a circle and sang `Happy Birthday'.

en_record_text:
It seems like years since Sunday morning. So much has happened — the whole world has turned upside down. But I'm alive, and that's the most important thing.
 On Sunday afternoon we heard that the Germans were going to take Father away. We know what that means — to a concentration camp. 
 `Mother's gone to ask Mr Van Daan about our hiding place,' said Margot. Mr Van Daan worked in the business with Daddy and is a good friend of his.

 Then Margot told me later that there was a mistake — the Germans had called her up, not Father. How can they take a girl of sixteen away from her family like that? But she's not going!

 A hiding place!! Where shall we hide? In the city?In the country? When, where, how ...?
 These questions were in my mind, though I couldn't ask them. Margot and I started to pack. I packed the craziest things! This diary first, then handkerchiefs, schoolbooks, a comb and some old letters.

en_record_text:
Miep and her husband Jan came to help and share the work. They carried some of the bags of clothes away for us. Miep and Jan work for Father's company and they are our close friends. I slept for the last night in my own bed, and Mummy woke me up at five-thirty. We dressed in lots of clothes. No Jew would dare to leave the house with a suitcase!
 At seven-thirty we left the house. I said goodbye to Moortje, my cat. The neighbours were going to look after her. We hurried to leave the house — we wanted to reach our hiding-place safely. It was the only thing that mattered.

en_record_text: The news is very bad today. The Germans are taking away many of our Jewish friends. They are sent to concentration camps at Westerbork, or even further away. We think many of them are murdered there. I feel terrible. The English radio says that the Germans are killing them with gas. Perhaps that's the quickest way to die. Perhaps you don't suffer so much that way.

For a long time now, I haven't really been interested in my schoolwork. The end of the war still seemed so far away. And if it isn't over by September, I won't go back to school, since I don't want to be two years behind. 
 Nothing but dreams and thoughts, until Saturday night when I felt terrible. I sat on the floor in my nightdress and said my prayers. Then I just lay down on the floor and cried. But I knew I had to fight against it and, finally, when I climbed back into bed at ten o'clock, the suffering was over! 
 And now it's really over. I've realized that I must do my schoolwork. I want to make something of my life. I want to be a journalist. I know I can write. A few of my stories are good, a lot of my diary is alive and amusing, but ... I don't know yet if I can be a really good writer. But then if I can't write books or for newspapers, I can always write for myself. I don't want to live like Mother, Mrs. Van Daan, and all the other women who simply do their work and are then forgotten. I need more than just a husband and children! I want to be useful, and to bring enjoyment to all people, even those that I've never met. I want to go on living after my death!
 I'm grateful to God for my writing. So I'll go on trying, and everything will be all right, because I'm not going to give up!

en_record_text:
It has reminded us that we are Jews, and that we must live like prisoners. We must forget our personal feelings and be brave and strong. One day this terrible war will be over. The time will come when we'll be people again and not just Jews! 
 Who has made us suffer like this? Who has separated us from all the other people? God has made us like this, but God will lift us up again. Perhaps afterwards, if there are any Jews left, our suffering will teach people something. Perhaps they will learn something about goodness, and this is why we have to suffer. We can never be just Dutch, or just English, we will always be Jews as well.

en_record_text:
You can imagine we often say, `Why are there wars? Why, oh why, can't people live together peacefully?' 
 No one can give a really good answer. Why is England making bigger and better aeroplanes and bombs, and at the same time also building new houses? Why do governments give millions each day for war, when they spend nothing on medicine or poor people? Why must people go without food, when there are mountains of food going bad in other parts of the world? Oh, why are people so crazy? 
 It's not only governments who make war. No, the common man is guilty too! We give our governments the authority to do it. There's something in people that makes them murder and kill. Unless all human beings change, there will still be wars. 
 I'm often sad here, but I still see our life in the Secret Annexe as an adventure. It's dangerous but exciting. I've decided that I want to live a different kind of life, not like other girls, and that I won't be an ordinary housewife. Living here is an interesting beginning to my life, and that's why I laugh at the amusing side of it, even when it's dangerous. I'm young, and I'm strong, happy and cheerful. I feel that I'm growing up more every day, and that the end of the war is not far away. Nature is still beautiful, and the people around me are good. Every day, I think what an interesting adventure this is! So why be sad or frightened?

 Acropolis,
 
 
 literally,
 means “the city on a hill.” Around two thousand and six
 hundred years ago,
 the place was chosen because, first of all,
 it would
 be difficult for the enemies of Athenians to attack the city on
 top of
 the hill. Moreover, people could also get
 an excellent view of the
 
 Mediterranean harbors.
 
 
 On top of the Acropolis was the temple of
 
 Parthenon
 
 
 which was built in honor of the Goddess Athena.On the south-hill of
 the Acropolis lies the Theatre of Dionysius.
 It is an outdoor theatre
 with stair like seats on three sides and the
 stage down in the centre.
 It is called
 an
 
 amphitheatre.
 
 
 The Greeks used to
 have play festivals where all the people of the city
 used to come together
 to watch plays.

 people started decorating the dead people like the mummies.

 Mummies.

 Zeus,

en_record_text:
Jhamak Ghimire, born with cerebral palsy in July 1980
 in Dhankuta district, taught herself to write with her left foot.
 Although she was not sent to school, she was always curious about
 learning and used to observe her sister when she was studying.
 Jhamak is, now, a celebrated Nepali author and a columnist at the
 Kantipur newspaper. She is the recipient of the prestigious Madan Puraskar
 Award for her autobiography “Jiwan Kanda Ki Phool”. In the same award-winning
 book, she writes first about the discrimination she faced due to her disability,
 and then eventually, how she managed to transcend that through her determination
 and love for knowledge.

subject: english
en_record_text:
I was a dumb, helpless and crippled girl who had all these capabilities snatched away
 from her. I was deprived of all this; denied everything. I was a helpless child deprived
 of all these; although my heart wished I could run along the slopes and walk around the
 lap of my nature, my feet failed me, they were powerless. How I wished I could talk to
 people around me, but was deprived of voice. All these desires will never be fulfilled
 now and they just fell heavily like a burden in my life.

 What’s more, our family’s financial position at the time wasn’t all that sound. So much
 so, it was difficult even to make the two ends meet. It was said that our parents would
 often go hungry for days on end, trying at best only to somehow feed us children. O! My
 grandma, that would not satisfy you and you would feed me till I would be full, ignoring
 your own hunger. Moreover, you put me to sleep with you and feed me time and again throughout
 the night. Ah, I remember how tasty were the foods that you fed me, my grandma!

 people around me, but was deprived of voice. All these desires will never be fulfilled 
 now and they just fell heavily like a burden in my life.
 
 What’s more, our family’s financial position at the time wasn’t all that sound. So much 
 so, it was difficult even to make the two ends meet. It was said that our parents would 
 often go hungry for days on end, trying at best only to somehow feed us children. O! My 
 grandma, that would not satisfy you and you would feed me till I would be full, ignoring 
 your own hunger. Moreover, you put me to sleep with you and feed me time and again throughout 
 the night. Ah, I remember how tasty were the foods that you fed me, my grandma!

subject: english
en_record_text:
I had also started seeing new colors in life. I used to feel that rays of hope lay in the
 letter of alphabet and, as such, their mastery became my life’s one and only ambition.
 I used to collect the pages of exercise books torn down and thrown away by my younger
 sister and go through the letters written therein all through the day-by copying them on
 the bare earth with the help of a twig, by inwardly repeating, memorizing them over and over
 again, many times without number. Time was giving me a strange kind of test. Even in dreams,
 I started seeing nothing but myself reading and writing. Whatever father used to teach my
 younger sister, I had already learnt by dint of my own endeavors and practices. I then started
 learning the composition of words- the exercise of forming words by combining the letters was
 by no means easy. I used to write all the letters of the alphabet on the ground at a time and
 then pick up the different ones to combine them into a word. For instance, kalama(कलम=pen),
 kala(कल=machine), phala(फल=fruit), jala(जल=water), etc .

 No more did my tiny toes shake and tremble as I wrote- they had been habituated to scrawl the
 letters. Like others catching hold of a pen with their hands, I used to catch hold of a twig and
 scrawl letters with the toes of any feet. To this day I write in a like manner. Like others using
 three fingers to hold a pen, I also used three toes of my feet while holding a pen or a pencil
 but people have the illusion that I use only two.

en_record_text:
I remember now that at the time I first learnt to write letters of the alphabet, I could not share
 the joy with anyone. I had, nevertheless, mastered the art of scrawling letters even if it was on the
 bare earth and had learnt to pronounce them only within my mind. The first day I had been able to
 scribble a letter of the consonant क (ka), I had sprayed a cloud of dust in the air out of sheer
 happiness because I had broken innumerable twigs time and again in order to learn writing this letter
 and I bruised the tender skin rubbing against the soil. Moreover, my toes bled when I practiced writing
 by dipping them on the dew drops collected on the bowl. If there ever lay any reason behind it, it was
 only to recognize the letters and to learn how to write them. When this lesson was learnt, I myself
 raised dust and sprayed it on my body. I had drawn a large-sized क (ka) on earth for everyone to see. But
 there was no question of anyone looking at it; instead some of them stepped on that letter and erased
 it. In other words, my first letter क (ka) was wiped out of existence even without anyone seeing it. 

 Alas! That day, when the letter that I joyfully scrawled was stepped over with shoes, my heart was badly torn to
 pieces. I expressed the feeling of distress by screaming, because the resulting pain was unbearable to me.
 That scream was perhaps an expression of resentment against those who had stepped over it.

 It’s true, there was no one to share my joys with, of learning the first letter of alphabet. I had shared
 that joy with this inert earth- I had celebrated the moment by spraying the dust all around; covering my own
 body with dust.

subject: english
en_record_text:
Be it the various rumors or the talks and discussions, they not only acted as a factor to encourage my adolescent
 heart, but also created in me a feeling that I must do something. No more was I subjected to negative comments about
 myself. The visitors to our house would not wish I was dead as it was the case earlier. To the contrary, they would
 extend their wishes for my long life and progress. I felt life was beautiful. The desire to live, once extinct, began
 budding up afresh.

 My literary journey was also taking its course. By the time, few of my poems and verse or lyrics had also got into the print.
 I no more have those periodicals in my collection. Nor do I recall their names. The one periodical I remember is Nawayuvaa
 monthly, which not only had a write-up published about me, but also helped reach my literary creations to many readers,
 admirers and well-wishers by having them published. I have always remained obliged to that publication.

 The appearance of one’s creations published in the media, I found, makes one feel extremely elated. On my part, I had also been so excited to see my first
composition that appeared in the print, that I fail to translate that moment of delight into words.

Mansarovar Lake is a freshwater lake located in Tibet. Lake Rakshastal is situated on the west of Mansarovar, while
 Mount Kailash lies to its north. At an altitude of 4,727 metres (15,510 feet), Lake Manasarowar is one of the highest
 freshwater lakes in the world. The word 'Manasarovara' originates from Sanskrit language, which is a combination of the words 'Manas' - mind and,
 'sarovara' - lake.

Rara Lake is the biggest lake of Nepal.
 It is located in Chhayanath Municipality in
 
 Mugu District,
 
 
 of
 
 Karnali Province,
 
 
 and is part of the
 
 Rara National Park.
 
 
 It lies at an altitude of about 2,990 metres (9,810 feet)
 above the sea level.
 Water from the lake flows into the
 
 Karnali river
 
 
 via the Nijar river.
 Animals like
 
 musk deer,
 
 
 
 red panda,
 
 
 
 yellow throated martin,
 
 
 
 Himalayan black bear
 
 
 and many other species are found inside the Rara National Park.
 With
 
 snow trout
 
 
 and other species of fish, Rara Lake also attracts
 a lot of migrant
 
 wildfowl,
 
 
 while the resident birds include
 
 Danfe
 
 
 (Impeyan Pheasant),
 the national bird of Nepal.

en_record_text:
Phoksundo Lake, also known as Shey Phoksundo Lake, is the deepest
 lake in Nepal.
 It lies within the
 
 Shey Phoksundo National Park
 
 
 in
 
 Dolpa district
 
 
 of Karnali Province.
 Phoksundo Lake lies at an altitude of 3,612 metres (11,850 feet) above the sea
 level.
 It is an
 
 alpine fresh water lake.
 
 
 There are more than 20
 
 stupas
 
 
 on the
 
 southern side of the lake,
 
 
 and one gompa in the eastern side. People around the lake follow ancient Tibetan
 
 Bon Po culture.
 
 
 The Lake is considered sacred by the
 
 local people of Dolpa.
 
 
 Often termed as the jewel of Dolpo, the beautiful Phoksundo Lake is
 surrounded by tall
 
 mountains.

en_record_text:
Tilicho lake is one of the highest lakes in the world. It lies at an altitude of 4,919 metres (16,138 feet)
 above the sea level. Located in
 Manang district 
 of Gandaki Province. 
 Tilicho Lake collects the
 glacial melt 
 of the northern slopes of the Annapurna range.
 Mountain lakes such as Tilicho that are caused by glacial activity are known as
 tarns. 
 Tarns are found mostly in the upper parts of the Himalayas.
 While walking along the
 Marshyangdi River 
 on the way to Tilicho Lake, people get great
 views of
 Manaslu 
 and
 Himalchuli 
 to the east.
ne_record_text:
Tilicho lake is one of the highest lakes in the world. It is 4,919 metres (16,138 feet) 
 high in the 
 Annapurna range 
 of the Himalayas in 
 Manang district, 
 Gandaki zone 
 of Nepal.
 Tilicho Lake collects the 
 glacial melt 
 of the entire northern slopes of Annapurna and the 
 Thorong Peak. 
 Mountain lakes that are caused by glacial activity are known as 
 tarns. 
 Tarns are found mostly in the upper reaches of the Himalaya, above 5,500 metres.
 In 2001, Hindu pilgrims from around the world visited the lake convinced that it is a 
 holy site mentioned in the great Hindu epic 
 Ramayan. 
 While walking along the 
 Marshyangdi River 
 on the way to Tilicho, people get to see great 
 views of 
 Manaslu 
 and 
 Himalchuli 
 to the east.
 As people go further north, they arrive at the beautiful 
 NarPhu Valleys 
 of Upper Manang.

en_record_text:
Phewa Lake is a freshwater lake located in
 Pokhara in
 Kaski district of
 Gandaki Province .
 It lies at an altitude of 742 metres (2,434 feet) above the sea level. On clear days, visitors can see the
 reflection of the
 Machhapuchhre 
 and other mountain peaks of the
 Annapurna
 and
 Dhaulagiri 
 ranges on the surface of the lake.
 There is a temple on a small island in the lake called the
 Barahi Temple .
 While the north shore of the lake has developed into a tourist area known
 as the
 Lake-Side ,
 the steep southwestern shore is still densely forested.

 and other mountain peaks of the

 the steep southwestern shore is still densely forested.

 Tibet. 
 At an altitude of 4,727 metres (15,510 feet) above the sea level, Lake Manasarowar is one of
 the highest freshwater lakes in the world.
 Another lake, Rakshastal, is situated on the west of Manasarovar, while Mount Kailash lies to its north.
 The word 'Manasarovara' originates
 from Sanskrit language, and is a combination of the words 'Manas' - mind and, 'Sarovara' - lake.
 According to
 Hindu scriptures 
 , anyone who drinks water from the lake is
 believed to be cleansed of all his or her sins.
 Pilgrims from India, Nepal, Tibet and the neighboring countries come to take
 ceremonial baths in the water of Manasarovar. Buddhists also consider
 the lake as sacred since
 Lord Buddha 
 is reported to have stayed and meditated near this
 lake on several occasions.

Mansarovar Lake is a freshwater lake located in

 Mount Kailash 
 lies to its north.
 At an altitude of 4,727 metres (15,510 feet), Lake Manasarowar is one of

 from Sanskrit language, which is a combination of the words 'Manas' - mind and, 'sarovara' - lake.
 According to the Hindu religion, the lake was first created in the mind of the
 Lord Brahma 
 after which it manifested on Earth.
 According to
 Hindu scriptures 
 ,anyone who drinks water from the lake is
 believed to be cleansed of all his sins committed over even a hundred lifetimes.
 Pilgrims from India, Nepal, Tibet and the neighboring countries come to take
 ceremonial baths in the cleansing water of the lake. Buddhists also consider
 the lake as sacred since
 Lord Buddha 
 supposedly stayed and meditated near this
 lake on several occasions.
 The lake is very popular in
 Buddhist literature 
 and associated with many
 teachings and stories in Buddhism.
 The lake has a few monasteries on its shores, 
 the most notable of which is the ancient 
 Chiu Gompa Monastery 
 built on a steep hill, which looks like it has been carved right out of the rock.

en_record_text: There have been different views on the use of zero from other civilizations. Zero implies there is no existence of anything. For example 56 is different from 560 or 506, which was the extension of zero from Indian civilization.

 Similarly, if the exponent is 'n',

en_record_text: Sets E and N denote the students who like English or Nepali respectively.These sets are made up of elements of the universal set U. So, we say, E is a subset of U Or, E ⊂ U N is a subset of U Or, N ⊂ U Here, E and N both have at least one element less than U. So, E and N are proper subsets of U.
ne_record_text: समूह E ले अङ्ग्रेजी र समूह N ले नेपाली मनपराउने विद्यार्थीहरूको समूहहरू जनाउँछन्। यी समूहहरू सर्वव्यापक समूह U का केही सदस्यहरूबाट निर्मित छन्। त्यसैले, हामीले E सर्वव्यापक समूह U को उपयुक्त उपसमूह भन्छौँ। यसलाई E ⊂ U लेखिन्छ। त्यसैगरी, N सर्वव्यापक समूह U को उपयुक्त उपसमूह हो। यसलाई N ⊂ U लेखिन्छ। यहाँ, E र N दुबैमा सर्वव्यापक समूह U भन्दा कम सदस्य छन्। त्यसैले, E र N सर्वव्यापक समूह U का उपयुक्त उपसमूहहरू हुन्।

subject: science
en_record_text:
Protozoa 
 The organisms that belong to this group are called single celled organisms because they are made up of only one cell. These organisms cannot be seen with bare eyes. 
 All the life processes in their body such as: eating, respiration, movement etc. are controlled by the single cell. 
 Reproduction takes place in them through binary fission. 
 These organisms are usually found on human bodies, sea water and in humid areas.
ne_record_text:
एककोषीय (Protozoa) 
 एउटा मात्र सूक्ष्म कोषले बनेको हुनाले यो समूहमा पर्ने प्राणीलाई एककोषीय जीव भनिन्छ। यिनीहरूलाई नाङ्गो आँखाले देख्न सकिँदैन । 
 यिनीहरूको शरीरमा सञ्चालन हुने सबै जीवन प्रक्रियाहरू जस्तै: खाना खाने, सास फेर्ने , एकबाट अर्को ठाउँमा सर्ने आदि काम एक मात्र कोषले नियन्त्रण गरेको हुन्छ। 
 यिनीहरूमा प्रजनन क्रिया कोषको विभाजनबाट हुन्छ । 
 यिनीहरू प्राय: मानिसको शरीर, समुन्द्रको पानी वा ओसिलो ठाउँमा पाइन्छन्।

subject: science
en_record_text:
Porifera 
 In Latin language porus means ‘pore’ i.e. hole and fer means 'to carry'. Since, organisms that belong to this group have holes all over their bodies, they are also called porifera (commonly known as sponges). 
 Their body is made up of many cells. 
 They do not have a head, eyes and limbs. 
 They are usually found in the saltwater of seas and oceans.
ne_record_text:
पोरिफेरा (Porifera) 
 ल्याटिन भाषामा porus भनेको 'pore' अर्थात् छिद्र र fer भनेको 'bearing' अर्थात् बोक्नु हो। त्यसैले शरीर भरी छिद्र नै छिद्र हुने भएकाले यिनीहरूलाई पोरिफेरा (वा स्पन्ज) पनि भनिन्छ। 
 धेरैओटा कोषहरू मिलेर यिनीहरूको शरीर बनेको हुन्छ। 
 यिनीहरूको शरीरमा टाउको, आँखा, खुट्टा आदिको विकास भएको हुँदैन। 
 यिनीहरू प्राय समुद्रको नुनिलो पानीमा बस्छन्।

subject: science
en_record_text:
Nemathelminthes 
 The organisms that belong to this group have flat bodies. 
 Their bodies are ribbon shaped. 
 They are parasites and live by sucking blood of other animals. 
 Tapeworm lives in the intestines of humans, cows, buffaloes and pigs. Similarly, liver fluke lives in the livers of cattle.
ne_record_text:
डोला किरा (Nemathelminthes) 
 यस समूहमा पर्ने जीवहरूको शरीर गोलो र लाम्चो आकारको हुन्छ। 
 यी जीवहरूको शरीरको अगाडी र पछाडीको भाग चुच्चो परेको हुन्छ। 
 यिनीहरूमा भाले र पोथी छुट्टिएका हुन्छन्। पोथीको तुलनामा भाले छोटो र सानो हुन्छ। यिनीहरूको मेलद्वारा नै प्रजनन हुन्छ। 
 यिनीहरूको शरीरमा मुख र मलद्वार हुन्छ। मानिसको शरीरभित्र पर्ने जुका (Ascaris) लगायत यस समूहमा पर्ने केही जीवहरू परजीवी हुन्।

subject: science

 सानो हुन्छ। यिनीहरूको मेलद्वारा नै प्रजनन हुन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text:
Annelida 
 The organisms that belong to this group have a long, segmented and cylindrical body. Their body is covered by soft and moist skin. 
 They have a food pipe in their body that joins their mouth with their anus. Most of the organisms in this group live in soil and water. Others like leech live as parasites and suck the blood of other animals. 
 Many creatures of this group breathe through skin and some breathe through gills. 
 They have blood vessels in their body and they also have a simple nervous system.
ne_record_text:
एनेलिडा (Annelida) 
 यी जीवहरूको शरीर खण्ड खण्ड परेको, लामो र गोलाकार हुने भएकोले यिनीहरूलाई एनेलिडा भनिन्छ। यिनीहरूको शरीर नरम र रसिलो छालाले ढाकेको हुन्छ। 
 यिनीहरूको शरीरमा मुखदेखि मलद्वारसम्म फैलने गरी लामो खाना नली हुन्छ। यिनीहरूमध्ये केही पानी र माटोमा हुर्कन्छन् भने जुगा जस्तो जीव अन्य प्राणीको रगत चुसेर परजीवीको रूपमा बाँच्छ। 
 यिनीहरूमध्ये धेरैले शरीरको बाहिरी सतहबाट सास फेर्छन् भने केहीले साधारण किसिमको फुल्का (Gills) को प्रयोग गर्छन्। 
 यिनीहरूको शरीरमा रक्तनली हुने भएकोले रक्तसञ्चार हुनुका साथै साधारण किसिमको मष्तिष्क प्रणालीको समेत विकास भएको हुन्छ।

ne_record_text: पाठको शिर्षकभित्र छिर्ने बित्तिकै तपाईले पाठ्यक्रम निर्देशित उदेश्य देख्नुहुनेछ। त्यसको तलपट्टि पाठभित्र के छ भन्ने कुराको सामान्य जानकारी पाउन सक्नुहुन्छ। अब ‘अगाडि बढ’ मा क्लिक गरी पाठभित्र छिर्नुहोस्। पहिलो पृष्ठ हेरेपछि अर्को पृष्ठमा जान चलिरहेको हरियो बटन वा पृष्ठ नम्बरमा क्लिक गर्नुहोस्। यहाँ चलिरहेको ठोस, तरल वा ग्यास मध्ये कुनै एकमा क्लिक गर्नुहोस्। जस्तै: ठोसमा क्लिक गरी भित्र गएपछि स्क्रिनको तलतिर देखिने तापक्रममा परिवर्तन, आयतनमा परिवर्तन, अवस्थामा परिवर्तन र रासायनिक परिवर्तन लेखेको बटनमा पालैपालो क्लिक गर्नुहोस्। X मा क्लिक गरी पहिलेकै पृष्ठमा फर्कनुहोस्। अब बाँकी तरल र ग्यासमा पनि यही प्रक्रिया दोहोर्याउनुहोस्। नयाँ पृष्ठमा गएर Sequence control button मा क्लिक गर्दै पढ्नुहोस्। साथै मिलिक मिलिक गर्ने ठाउँमा कर्सर लगेर थर्मोमिटरको नामाकरण हेर्नुहोस्। पुन: नयाँ पेजमा जानुहोस् र सिक्सज (Six's) थर्मोमिटरको बारेमा जान्न Sequence control button क्लिक गर्नुहोस्। फेरि मिलिक मिलिक गर्ने ठाउँमा कर्सर लगेर थर्मोमिटरको नामाकरण हेर्नुहोस् र Sequence control button क्लिक गर्दै जानुहोस्। अब थर्मोमिटरलाई राम्ररी अवलोकन गरेर न्यूनतम र अधिकतम तापक्रमलाई खाली ठाउँमा टाइप गर्नुहोस्। अब रिसेट लेखेको बटनमा क्लिक गरी पुन: Sequence control button दबाउनुहोस्। समय परिवर्तन भएको हेर्न स्क्रिनको तल घुमिरहेको बटनमा क्लिक गर्नुहोस्। फेरि न्यूनतम र अधिकतम तापक्रम टाइप गर्नुहोस्। फेरि खेल्न सङ्केतमा क्लिक गर्नुहोस्। अब नयाँ पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। स्क्रिनमा देखिएका चित्रहरूलाई मिल्ने तापक्रमको खाली कोठामा तानेर राख्दै माथिको जानकारी पढ्नुहोस्। फेरि खेल्नको लागी सङ्केतमा क्लिक गर्नुहोस्।

subject: science
en_record_text: Divide the children into three different groups in order to show the changes in matters due to heat. Now let them be involved in the activity related to solid, liquid and gas. For examples; carry out an iron ball and ring experiment in which ball is expected to pass through a ring before and after heating it. This activity proves that volume of solid increases due to heat. To prove that heat increases volume of a liquid, let them boil milk and tell them to observe the volume before and after milk boils. Similarly, to show them that volume of gas increases due to heat, take an empty bottle, water trough and a balloon. Now, put that balloon tightly on the mouth of a bottle. Then keep that bottle into the warm water for some time. Ask them to check if the size of that balloon is changed. Let them discuss on the reason behind it. Besides, carry out further more activities if needed and make sure that all groups have equal chance to work on each stall of solid, liquid and gas. As soon as children are done, let them discuss and write a conclusion of the experiments.
ne_record_text: तापको असरले पदार्थमा हुने परिवर्तन देखाउनको लागि विद्यार्थीहरूलाई तीन समूहमा विभाजन गरी  ठोस ,तरल र ग्याससम्बन्धी  क्रियाकलाप गराउनुहोस्। जस्तै: तापले ठोसको आयतनमा वृद्दि गर्छ भन्ने कुरा अझै प्रष्ट बनाउन फलामको रिङमा फलामकै गुच्चा छिराउने र ताप दिएपछि पनि पुन: सोही क्रियाकलाप दोहोर्याउन लगाउनुहोस्। तापले तरल पदार्थको आयतनमा वृद्दि गर्छ भन्ने कुरा बुझाउनको लागि दुध उमाल्न लगाउनुहोस् र सुरु र अन्तिम अवस्थाबीच दुधको आयतनमा देखिएको परिवर्तन अवलोकन गर्न लगाउनुहोस्।  त्यसैगरी तापले ग्यासको आयतनमा वृद्दि गर्छ भन्ने कुरा बुझाउनको लागि खाली बोतलको मुखमा बिर्को खोलेर बेलुनको मुख छिराउनुहोस्। अब उक्त बोतललाई तातो पानी भएको ठाउँमा केहीबेर सम्म राखेर राम्ररी अवलोकन गर्न लगाउनुहोस्। बेलुनको आकार मा के परिवर्तन आयो र त्यसको कारण के होला त भन्ने बारे छलफल चलाउनुहोस्। यी बाहेक पनि आवश्यकता  र अवस्था हेरी तपाईले क्रियाकलापहरू बढाउन सक्नुहुन्छ। अब सबै विद्यार्थीले ठोस, तरल र ग्यासमा काम गरून् भन्नको लागि चक्रिय रूपमा उनीहरूलाई प्रत्येक क्रियाकलापमा पुग्न सक्ने वातावरण मिलाईदिनुहोस्। सबै क्रियाकलाप सकिए पश्चात् विद्यार्थीहरूसँग छलफल गरी सारांश लेख्न लगाउनुहोस्।

ne_record_text: यहाँ सिसीको बिर्को धेरै नै कस्सिएकोले खोल्नै सकिएन। त्यसैले, ठोस वस्तुलाई ताप दिँदा त्यस वस्तुको आयतनमा बृद्दि हुन्छ भन्ने कुरालाई प्रमाणित गर्न मनतातो पानीमा सिसीको बिर्को डुब्ने गरी केहिबेर राख्ने। यसले गर्दा बिर्कोको आयतनमा बृद्दि हुन्छ। त्यसपछि त बिर्को सजिलै खुलिन्छ।

en_record_text: Alcohol is kept in the thermometer to measure minimum temperature. When the temperature of air falls down, alcohol contracts and the vacuum created is replaced by mercury.
ne_record_text: न्यून तापक्रम नाप्नको लागि थर्मोमिटरमा अल्कोहल राखिएको हुन्छ। वायुको तापक्रम घट्दा अल्कोहलको आयतन संकुचित (contract) हुन्छ र घटेको आयतनको ठाउँ ओगट्न माथिल्लो स्तरको मर्करी तल्लो स्तरमा प्रसारित हुन्छ। मर्करीसँगै सुचक पनि तल झर्छ। यसरी थर्मोमिटरमा भएको सुचकले अल्कोहल भएको भागमा न्यूनतम तापक्रम रेकर्ड गर्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: When temperature of an environment increases, expansion on the volume of half filled alcohol takes place. As a result, mercury inside the thermometer is pushed forward to the new level. Indicator also moves along with it. Hence, lower limit of an indicator inside the thermometer indicates new record for the maximum temperature.

subject: science
en_record_text: Light a candle. Call for an interested volunteer among the participants to set a fire on one incense stick keeping just above but untouched with the flame. Now ask them all to observe an experiment very carefully. Similarly, ask another volunteer to hold a spoon in one end and heat the other end. Let all children experience it one after another. ‘What happened?’ ask them. Please make sure that children follow safety measures while working with fire. Now, ask them to keep their hand near that candle. At the same time, warn them not to touch fire. In this way, set a discussion on which processes were involved to transfer heat in all three different experiments? If possible, take a magnifying glass to focus light on a thin sheet of paper so that it burns easily. It helps them know that radiation does not require any medium to transfer heat.
ne_record_text: एउटा मैनबत्ती बाल्नुहोस्। त्यस मैनबत्तीको सीधा ठिक माथि तर आगोको ज्वालाले नभेट्ने गरी कुनै एक विद्यार्थीलाई धूपको टुप्पो राख्न लगाई बाँकी सबैलाई राम्ररी अवलोकन गर्न लगाउनुहोस्। त्यसैगरी अब चम्चाको एउटा छेऊमा समाती अर्को छेउ उक्त मैनबत्तीमा तताउन लगाई राम्ररी अनुभव सहितको अवलोकन गर्न लगाउनुहोस। तातो वस्तु छुँदा विद्यार्थीहरूलाई सावधानी अपनाउनको लागि सचेत गराउनुहोस्। अबभने मैनबत्तीलाई नछोई त्यसको नजिकै हात राख्दा के हुन्छ भन्ने कुरा थाहा पाउन सबैलाई हात राखेरै अनुभव गर्न लगाउनुहोस्। यसरी काम गर्दा तापको प्रसारण कुन कुन विधिद्वारा भयो भन्ने कुरा अवगत गराउनुहोस्। सम्भव भए Magnifying glass को प्रयोग गरी सूर्यको किरणलाई पातलो कागजमा केन्द्रीकृत गरी जलाउने प्रक्रियाद्वारा विकिरणमा विना माध्यम ताप प्रसारण हुन्छ भन्ने कुरा बुझाउन सकिन्छ।

ne_record_text: पाठको शिर्षकभित्र छिर्ने बित्तिकै तपाईले पाठ्यक्रम निर्देशित उदेश्य देख्नुहुनेछ। त्यसको तलपट्टि पाठभित्र के छ भन्ने कुराको सामान्य जानकारी पाउन सक्नुहुन्छ। अब ‘अगाडि बढ’ मा क्लिक गरी पाठभित्र छिर्नुहोस्। पहिलो पृष्ठमा जनावर र बिरूवाबीच पाइने भिन्नबताहरू दिइएका छन्।Sequence control button प्रयोग गरी सबै भिन्नरताहरू हेर्नुहोस्। तपाईले अन्य भिन्नeताहरू पनि थप्न सक्नुहुनेछपढेका कुरा फेरि दोहोर्या उनको लागि सङ्केतमा क्लिक गर्नुहोस्। बटनमा क्लिक गरी नयाँ पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। जनावरलाई शरीरमा ढाड भएका र नभएका गरी दुई भागमा विभाजन गरेको पृष्ठ हेरेर पुन: नयाँ पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। ढाड भएका जनावरहरूको चित्रलाई पालैपालो बीचको कोठामा तानेर राख्नुहोस्।पढेका कुरा फेरि दोहोर्यानउनको लागि सङ्केतमा क्लिक गर्नुहोस्। अब नयाँ पृष्ठमा पुगेर स्क्रिनमा देखिने चित्रलाई मिल्ने समूहमा तानेर राख्नुहोस्।त्यसपछि पाँचै समूहमा पालैपालो क्लिक गर्दै Sequence control button प्रयोग गरी हेर्नुहोस्। Sequence control button नजिकै रहेका बटनहरू पनि क्लिक गरी हेर्न नभुल्नुहोला। जनावरका विशेषता बताउने खेल खेल्नको लागि नयाँ पृष्ठमा जाऔँ। यस पृष्ठमा दिइएका जनावरका विशेषताहरूलाई तानेर मिल्ने समूहको बाकसमा राख्नुहोस्। सङ्केतमा क्लिक गरी फेरि खेल्नुहोस्। भ्यागुताको जीवनचक्र हेर्नको लागि नयाँ पृष्ठमा जानुहोस्। चरणबद्द रूपमा एकपछि अर्को गर्दै भ्यागुताको जीवन प्रक्रियाका केही चित्रहरूलाई पहेँलो कोठामा तानेर राख्दै पढ्नुहोस्।

en_record_text: Mammals and birds are warm-blooded, which means that they can make their own body heat even when it is cold outside. Whether it is sunny and hot outside or there is a snowstorm and it is very cold, warm-blooded animals have body temperatures that usually stay the same. For example, human being have an approximately 98.6 0C body temperature.
ne_record_text: चरा र स्तनधारी समूहमा पर्ने जनावरका शरीरमा तातो रगत हुन्छ। यसको मतलब बाहिर वातावरण चिसो भएपनि यी जनावरहरूले आफ्नो शरीरलाई तातो बनाईराख्छन्। चाहे वातावरणमा अति गर्मी होस् या अति जाडो, यीनीहरूको शरीरको तापक्रम स्थिर रहन्छ। जस्तै: जीवित मानिसको शरीरको तापक्रम सामान्यतया जतिबेला पनि 98.6 डीग्री फरेनहाईट हुन्छ।

subject: science
en_record_text: Cold-blooded animals, like reptiles, amphibians, and fish, become hotter and colder, depending on the temperature outside.
